channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump postpones threatened strikes on Iranian power plants, cites ongoing talks
item.description: President Trump said the U.S. will delay military strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure while it negotiates with Iran.
item.content:encoded: President Trump said the U.S. will delay military strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure while it negotiates with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Former U.S. envoy to Iran discusses what's next for talks between the US and Iran
item.description: For a sense of the future of indirect talks between the U.S. and Iran, NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Robert Malley, former U.S. envoy to Iran.
item.content:encoded: For a sense of the future of indirect talks between the U.S. and Iran, NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Robert Malley, former U.S. envoy to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Markwayne Mullin confirmed as the next secretary of Homeland Security
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Sen. Markwayne Mullin to serve as the next secretary of Homeland Security. The Oklahoma Republican replaces Kristi Noem and takes over a department mired in controversy.
item.content:encoded: The Senate has confirmed Sen. Markwayne Mullin to serve as the next secretary of Homeland Security. The Oklahoma Republican replaces Kristi Noem and takes over a department mired in controversy.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Trump takes aim at windmills despite increasing energy costs
item.description: President Trump's mission to fight renewable wind energy comes at a time of rising energy costs.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's mission to fight renewable wind energy comes at a time of rising energy costs.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war in Iran, postponing threatened strikes on its power plants, but Iran denies such talks happened; ICE agents were deployed to U.S. airports Monday.
item.content:encoded: Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war in Iran, postponing threatened strikes on its power plants, but Iran denies such talks happened; ICE agents were deployed to U.S. airports Monday.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Billy Idol talks about his pop stardom, rock 'n' roll excess and new doc about his life
item.description: Billy Idol was punk in the 1970s, a pop star in the '80s, and now his rock 'n' roll excess is the subject of a Hulu documentary called Billy Idol Should Be Dead. It begins streaming on March 26.
item.content:encoded: Billy Idol was punk in the 1970s, a pop star in the '80s, and now his rock 'n' roll excess is the subject of a Hulu documentary called Billy Idol Should Be Dead. It begins streaming on March 26.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Iranians leaving the country share their thoughts on US-Israeli strikes
item.description: At Iran's border, those fleeing the war speak of an unbearable choice: endure the regime or risk everything to see it fall.
item.content:encoded: At Iran's border, those fleeing the war speak of an unbearable choice: endure the regime or risk everything to see it fall.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Relatives and advocates seek answers after Afghan asylum seeker dies in ICE custody
item.description: Family members and U.S. veterans' advocates await answers about the death in ICE custody of an Afghan asylum seeker who aided U.S. Army Special Forces during the war in Afghanistan.
item.content:encoded: Family members and U.S. veterans' advocates await answers about the death in ICE custody of an Afghan asylum seeker who aided U.S. Army Special Forces during the war in Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider

item.title: NCAA president talks about new rules that allow college athletes to be paid
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks NCAA President Charlie Baker about the first year in which college athletes are paid to play.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks NCAA President Charlie Baker about the first year in which college athletes are paid to play.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump says the US is in talks with Iran to end the war, which Iran denies
item.description: Trump's claim of "very good" talks with Iran contrasts sharply with Tehran's denial, as escalating tensions and backchannel diplomacy raise fears of a wider war in the Gulf.
item.content:encoded: Trump's claim of "very good" talks with Iran contrasts sharply with Tehran's denial, as escalating tensions and backchannel diplomacy raise fears of a wider war in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy
